project.name: claude-control
project.description: A Claude Code plugin providing a development dashboard with story tracking, real-time WebSocket updates, and a server daemon.

nodes.name: marketplace.json
nodes.summary: Local marketplace registry that registers the claude-control plugin (v0.1.0) as a locally-sourced observer-only workflow visualizer for Claude Code.

nodes.name: plugin.json
nodes.summary: Plugin manifest declaring the claude-control plugin's metadata, entry points for commands, hooks, and skills used by the Claude Code plugin loader.

nodes.name: constitution.md
nodes.summary: Project constitution codifying four core engineering principles (Think Before Coding, Simplicity First, Surgical Changes, Goal-Driven Execution) plus project-specific rules including mandatory TDD, verification gates, and commit discipline.

nodes.name: biome.json
nodes.summary: Biome linter and formatter configuration for the monorepo, enabling VCS integration, import organization, linting with recommended rules, and tab-based formatting.

nodes.name: DESIGN.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive design system reference inspired by Supabase, covering color palette, typography, component styling, layout principles, depth/elevation, responsive behavior, and an agent prompt guide for consistent UI generation.

nodes.name: pnpm-workspace.yaml
nodes.summary: pnpm workspace definition declaring all packages under the packages/ directory as workspace members for monorepo dependency management.

nodes.name: tsconfig.base.json
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript base configuration providing common compiler options (bundler module resolution, strict mode) extended by workspace packages.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Developer guide for the claude-control plugin covering local development setup, verification steps, and instructions for running tests and the background daemon directly.

nodes.name: hooks.json
nodes.summary: Plugin hook registration configuration mapping all Claude Code lifecycle events (SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, Notification, UserPromptSubmit, etc.) to the bootstrap and forward shell scripts.
nodes.languageNotes: SessionStart uses bootstrap.sh (synchronous); all other events use forward.sh, with async=true on non-blocking paths.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Minimal skill definition file declaring the claude-control skill entry point used by the Claude Code plugin skill loader.

nodes.name: claude-control-dashboard-hierarchy.html
nodes.summary: Self-contained static HTML prototype of the Claude Control dashboard, embedding full CSS and JavaScript to render the stories board, plan view, standup digest, and sidebar navigation without a build step.
nodes.languageNotes: 2535-line single-file prototype with inline styles and scripts; serves as the visual ground truth reference for the production React implementation.

nodes.name: claude-control-handoff.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive design handoff document for the Claude Control dashboard covering purpose, information architecture, five view specifications, component library, design tokens, data model mapping, and open questions for the spec phase.

nodes.name: PRD.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Product Requirements Document for Claude Control, defining the observer-only plugin that provides a workflow visualizer and agile layer on top of Claude Code + Superpowers, covering goals, surfaces, hook event model, and delivery phases.

nodes.name: 2026-05-06-claude-control-foundation.md
nodes.summary: Detailed implementation plan for the Claude Control foundation (Weeks 1–2), covering 10 tasks including monorepo scaffolding, shared Zod event schemas, SQLite migrations, the security gate, hook handlers, HTTP server routes, daemon lifecycle, integration tests, and plugin scaffold.

nodes.name: 2026-05-16-phase2-superpowers-integration.md
nodes.summary: Phase 2 implementation plan for Superpowers integration and the Stories layer, covering 16 tasks including plan/story parsers, checkbox diff, SuperpowersWatcher, StoryService, WebSocket server, REST API routes, and slash commands.

nodes.name: 2026-05-17-phase3-dashboard.md
nodes.summary: Phase 3 implementation plan for the React dashboard including StandupService, HandoffService, API routes, the full web package scaffold with Vite/Zustand/React Router, all SPA views (Plan, Stories, Story, Spec, Standup), and the SPA catch-all serving integration.

nodes.name: 2026-05-21-filter-stories-by-status.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for the filter-stories-by-status feature, detailing 5 tasks: adding filter state to useUiStore, CSS for filter pills, building the FilterBar component, modifying KanbanColumn with an isFiltered prop, and wiring StoriesPage.

nodes.name: 2026-05-23-fix-auth-token-url-exposure.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for fixing auth token URL exposure, covering 6 tasks: removing query-param fallback from checkAuth, first-message WebSocket auth, removing token from WebSocket URL in the browser hook, reading the bootstrap token from the URL hash, updating the plugin open command, and end-to-end verification.

nodes.name: 2026-05-06-claude-control-foundation-design.md
nodes.summary: Foundation design spec covering the monorepo structure, Hooks API event model, shared Zod schemas, daemon architecture, plugin scaffold, SQLite schema, testing strategy, and dev workflow for the Claude Control project.

nodes.name: 2026-05-13-superpowers-integration-stories-design.md
nodes.summary: Phase 2 design spec for Superpowers integration and the Stories layer, detailing plan parser, checkbox diff, SuperpowersWatcher, phase inference, story format with frontmatter, StoryService, WebSocket protocol, REST API endpoints, and success criteria.

nodes.name: 2026-05-17-phase3-dashboard-design.md
nodes.summary: Phase 3 design spec for the dashboard frontend and backend additions, covering the SPA architecture, token/port discovery, WebSocket integration, all frontend views, StandupService, HandoffService, API routes, and testing approach.

nodes.name: 2026-05-20-filter-stories-by-status-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for the filter-stories-by-status feature, specifying the useUiStore filter state slice, FilterBar component with pill tabs and count badges, KanbanColumn isFiltered prop, StoriesPage wiring, visual design, and behaviour rules.

nodes.name: 2026-05-23-fix-auth-token-url-exposure-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for fixing auth token exposure in the browser URL, describing the problem of tokens appearing in query params, the proposed solution of moving the token to the URL hash, and the component-by-component changes required across the plugin, web, and server packages.

nodes.name: US-2026-05-19-filter-stories-by-status-on-the-board-vi.md
nodes.summary: User story card for filtering stories by status on the Kanban board, including acceptance criteria for the filter pill UI and filtered column empty state.

nodes.name: sessions
nodes.summary: Core sessions table recording each Claude Code session with its working directory, model, agent type, permission mode, timestamps, active/ended status, and inferred Superpowers phase.

nodes.name: events
nodes.summary: Events table storing all hook events fired by Claude Code, keyed to a session and subagent, with JSON payload and timestamp, indexed for efficient session and event-name lookups.

nodes.name: stories
nodes.summary: Stories table linking file paths to parsed story metadata (title, size, status, linked spec/plan paths) with timestamps, indexed by status for the Kanban board filter feature.

nodes.name: plan_tasks
nodes.summary: Plan tasks table recording parsed task headings and their associated file lists from implementation plan documents, indexed by plan path.

nodes.name: plan_steps
nodes.summary: Plan steps table tracking individual checkbox items within plan tasks, including their completion state, completion timestamp, and the inferred hook event that triggered completion.

nodes.name: handoffs
nodes.summary: Handoffs table recording generated handoff documents associated with stories, tracking the file path and generation timestamp for retrieval in the dashboard.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration for the shared package, extending a shared base config and including only the src directory.

nodes.name: standup.ts
nodes.summary: Mounts the standup API route, generating a daily standup digest for a given date via the StandupService.

nodes.name: stories.ts
nodes.summary: Mounts HTTP route handlers for the stories CRUD API, supporting list, create, get, update, and archive operations on story records.

nodes.name: mountStoryRoutes
nodes.summary: Routes story API requests implementing full CRUD: list all, create, get by ID, patch update, and archive operations.

nodes.name: superpowers.ts
nodes.summary: Mounts HTTP route handlers for reading superpowers plan and spec documents, with path validation to prevent directory traversal.

nodes.name: mountSuperpowersRoutes
nodes.summary: Routes superpowers plan and spec API requests, validating paths and returning parsed plan or spec body content.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Implements HandoffService which generates Markdown handoff documents from story and plan data, persisting them to disk and the database, and lists all generated handoffs.

nodes.name: HandoffService
nodes.summary: Service class that generates structured handoff Markdown documents combining story metadata, plan summaries, and story body content, then writes them to disk and records them in the database.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Handles incoming Claude Code hook events by parsing and validating the payload, triggering superpowers file-change detection for Write events, and dispatching the event to the store and bus.

nodes.name: handleHookEvent
nodes.summary: Validates and processes an incoming hook event: triggers file-change handlers for Write events and dispatches all events to the store and bus.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Provides daemon lifecycle utilities: writing the runtime PID/port JSON file, managing an idle-shutdown timer that stops the server after inactivity, and registering graceful SIGTERM/SIGINT shutdown handlers.

nodes.name: writeRuntimeJson
nodes.summary: Writes the daemon runtime metadata (port, pid, token) as JSON to the data directory for CLI client discovery.

nodes.name: startIdleTimer
nodes.summary: Creates a resettable idle timer that triggers a graceful server shutdown after a configurable period of inactivity.

nodes.name: registerShutdownHandler
nodes.summary: Registers SIGTERM and SIGINT process signal handlers that perform graceful shutdown by closing the database and HTTP server.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Provides request authentication via bearer token and localhost-only host header checks, plus a sliding-window RateLimiter class for per-route request throttling.

nodes.name: RateLimiter
nodes.summary: Sliding-window rate limiter that tracks request counts per key within a configurable time window to enforce per-route throttling.

nodes.name: checkAuth
nodes.summary: Validates incoming requests by enforcing localhost-only Host headers and requiring a matching bearer token for non-localhost origins.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Implements StandupService which queries the database to generate a daily standup report including shipped stories, work-in-progress items, and detected blockers from repeated tool failures.

nodes.name: StandupService
nodes.summary: Service class that generates a structured daily standup digest by querying done, in-progress, and blocked stories from the database for a given date.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Provides database write helpers for session lifecycle and event persistence: upsertSession, endSession, and persistEvent, which upserts a session and writes each hook event with its full payload to the events table.

nodes.name: persistEvent
nodes.summary: Upserts the session record and writes a hook event row to the database, also ending the session on StopSession events.

nodes.name: migrate.ts
nodes.summary: Implements runMigrations which reads SQL migration files from a directory in sorted order and applies any not yet recorded in the _migrations tracking table.

nodes.name: runMigrations
nodes.summary: Reads .sql files from the migrations directory, skips already-applied ones tracked in _migrations, and executes new migrations transactionally.

nodes.name: template.ts
nodes.summary: Provides the scaffoldStory template function that generates the initial Markdown content with YAML frontmatter for a newly created story file.

nodes.name: scaffoldStory
nodes.summary: Generates the initial Markdown story file content with YAML frontmatter fields for id, title, status, size, and creation date.

nodes.name: diff.ts
nodes.summary: Provides diffCheckboxState which compares two arrays of parsed checkbox states to produce a list of checkbox changes (index, old value, new value) for plan progress tracking.

nodes.name: diffCheckboxState
nodes.summary: Compares two checkbox-state arrays and returns a list of changed positions with their old and new checked values.

nodes.name: parser.ts
nodes.summary: Exports parsePlan (re-exported from an external dependency) and parseSpec, a function that extracts the spec title from a Markdown heading.

nodes.name: phase.ts
nodes.summary: Provides advancePhase and inferPhase utilities for managing the superpowers workflow phase: advancing phase order and inferring the current phase from recent file-write events in the database.

nodes.name: inferPhase
nodes.summary: Queries recent file-write events from the database to infer the current superpowers workflow phase based on which file paths were written.

nodes.name: standup-handoff.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the standup and handoff API endpoints, exercising report generation and document output through the main server entry point.

nodes.name: service.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the handoff service, validating document generation logic from story and event data seeded into an in-memory SQLite database.

nodes.name: seedStoryFile
nodes.summary: Test helper that writes a story markdown file to disk and inserts a matching story row into the database, establishing preconditions for handoff service tests.

nodes.name: observer-contract.test.ts
nodes.summary: Contract tests for the hooks observer module, verifying that hook events are captured and published to the event bus without modifying any Claude Code behavior.

nodes.name: phase-inference.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the phase inference logic in the hooks module, asserting that the correct workflow phase is derived from sequences of hook events on the bus.

nodes.name: collectPhaseEvents
nodes.summary: Test helper that subscribes to the bus and collects all phase-related events into an array for assertion.

nodes.name: security.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the security middleware, verifying token validation, header checks, and that unauthorized requests are correctly rejected.

nodes.name: makeRequest
nodes.summary: Test helper that constructs and dispatches an HTTP request with configurable headers and overrides for security middleware testing.

nodes.name: service.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the standup service, covering report generation from stories and blocker events seeded into an in-memory SQLite database.

nodes.name: seedStory
nodes.summary: Test helper that inserts a story record with configurable status and timestamps into the database for standup service test scenarios.

nodes.name: seedBlockerEvents
nodes.summary: Test helper that bulk-inserts hook events marking permission blockers for a given story and session, used to exercise blocker detection in standup reports.

nodes.name: store.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the data store module, covering CRUD operations and query correctness against an in-memory SQLite database initialized with migrations.

nodes.name: diff.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the superpowers diff module, verifying plan comparison and change detection logic across various task step configurations.

nodes.name: makePlan
nodes.summary: Test fixture factory that constructs a structured plan object from an array of task step descriptions for use in diff test cases.

nodes.name: parser.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the superpowers parser module, checking correct extraction of structured data from Claude hook event payloads.

nodes.name: phase.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the superpowers phase module, verifying phase detection and state transitions from raw hook events stored in the database.

nodes.name: events.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the `HookEventSchema` Zod schema covering all Claude Code hook event types (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, etc.) with discriminated unions. The canonical runtime-validated event type definition for the shared package.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zod discriminated unions with z.literal for exhaustive hook event type coverage.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel entry point for the shared package, re-exporting all public types and functions from events, plan, story, and api modules.

nodes.name: plan.ts
nodes.summary: Defines plan-related types (`ParsedPlan`, `PlanTask`, `PlanStep`) and exports the `parsePlan` function that parses markdown plan documents into structured task/step objects.

nodes.name: parsePlan
nodes.summary: Parses a markdown plan document into a structured `ParsedPlan` object with tasks, steps, and file references by iterating lines and matching heading/checkbox patterns.

nodes.name: events.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for the `HookEventSchema` Zod schema, covering parse success and failure cases for all hook event variants.

nodes.name: plan.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for the `parsePlan` function, verifying correct extraction of tasks, steps, file lists, and completion status from markdown plan documents.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry document declaring the root `<div id='root'>` mount point and loading the React application via `src/main.tsx`.

nodes.name: ui.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store slice managing UI state (e.g., active sidebar item or modal visibility) for the web dashboard.

nodes.name: ws.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store slice holding WebSocket connection state including connection status, active session ID, current phase, and active story ID, exposed via `useWsStore`.

nodes.name: postcss.config.js
nodes.summary: Minimal PostCSS configuration enabling the Tailwind CSS plugin for the web package.

nodes.name: tailwind.config.js
nodes.summary: Tailwind CSS configuration scoping content paths to the web package source files and enabling any custom theme extensions.

nodes.name: tsconfig.tsbuildinfo
nodes.summary: TypeScript incremental compilation cache file generated by `tsc --build`; records file hashes and dependency graph for faster subsequent builds.

nodes.name: SizePill.tsx
nodes.summary: Small pill badge component that displays a story size label (e.g., S/M/L/XL) with color-coded styling and an optional click handler.

nodes.name: StatusPill.tsx
nodes.summary: Small pill badge component that displays a story status label with color-coded styling and an optional click handler for status cycling.

nodes.name: StepRow.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared row component that renders a single plan step with its label, state indicator (done/active/pending), and optional timestamp.

nodes.name: TaskCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Expandable task card component that renders a task's overall state (done/in-progress/pending) derived from its steps, with a collapsible step list rendered via StepRow.

nodes.name: TaskCard
nodes.summary: Exported React component rendering an expandable task card with step-level progress and collapsible details.

nodes.name: TypeIcon.tsx
nodes.summary: Tiny shared component that renders an emoji or icon character representing an artifact type (story, spec, plan, etc.).

nodes.name: StandupSection.tsx
nodes.summary: Standup digest section component that renders a labeled list of standup items (shipped/in-progress/blockers) with story ID and detail formatting.

nodes.name: StatsGrid.tsx
nodes.summary: Simple stats grid component that renders a row of key-value stat tiles from a provided stats array for the standup dashboard summary.

nodes.name: StandupPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Standup dashboard page that fetches the daily digest and renders shipped/in-progress/blocker sections with a clipboard copy action for markdown export.

nodes.name: StandupPage
nodes.summary: Exported React page component that fetches the standup digest and renders section components with a copy-to-clipboard action.

nodes.name: formatDigest
nodes.summary: Utility function that serializes a standup digest object into a copyable markdown string with dated header and three categorized sections.

nodes.name: KanbanColumn.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the KanbanColumn component, verifying story rendering, filtered empty-state display, and clear-filter interaction with the UI store.

nodes.name: ui-store.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the UI Zustand store, verifying initial state and setStoryFilter action behavior.

nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code project-level settings enabling Bash/git/node/WebSearch permissions, PreToolUse audit logging hooks for Bash and Write/Edit tools, and a list of enabled Claude Code plugins including claude-mem, frontend-design, pyright-lsp, and understand-anything.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs on push to main and pull requests, installing dependencies with Bun and executing both the server (bun test) and web (bun run test) test suites.

nodes.name: release.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions release workflow triggered on version tags or manual dispatch that runs tests, extracts the changelog entry via scripts/extract-changelog.mjs, and creates a GitHub Release with the extracted notes.

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Git ignore rules excluding node_modules, build artifacts, runtime files, and environment secrets from version control.

nodes.name: .release-it.json
nodes.summary: Release-it configuration using conventional-changelog preset to auto-generate CHANGELOG.md entries, create git tags, and run scripts/sync-version.mjs after each version bump to propagate the version across all workspace packages.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root workspace package.json for the claude-control monorepo, declaring Bun workspace members under packages/*, root dev scripts (dev, build, test, lint, release), and dev dependencies including Biome, release-it, and TypeScript.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the @cc/server workspace package, declaring its scripts (dev with watch, test with bun:test), runtime dependencies, and dev dependencies for the Bun HTTP daemon.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the server package, extending a base tsconfig with compiler options targeted at Bun's runtime environment.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the @cc/shared workspace package that exposes shared TypeScript type definitions, declaring its main entry, exports map, and build scripts.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the @cc/web React dashboard, listing React, Vite, TailwindCSS, and vitest as dependencies and declaring build, dev, preview, and test scripts.

nodes.name: bunfig.toml
nodes.summary: Bun configuration for the web package specifying the test runner settings so that bun test resolves correctly in the web workspace.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the React web dashboard, enabling strict mode, JSX transform, and DOM type libraries for a Vite-based frontend build.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite configuration for the web dashboard that sets up the React plugin, configures a dev-server proxy forwarding /api and /ws to the claude-control daemon port (default 47821), and configures vitest with jsdom and global test setup.
nodes.languageNotes: The WebSocket proxy (ws: true) is essential for real-time dashboard updates through the daemon.

nodes.name: plugin.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code plugin manifest declaring the claude-control plugin's name, description, version, author, homepage, and repository for registration in the Claude Code plugin marketplace.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Project changelog tracking version history; currently documents the initial 1.0.0 release. Consumed programmatically by scripts/extract-changelog.mjs to populate GitHub Release notes.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project guide for Claude Code AI assistants covering the Bun-only package manager requirement, monorepo workspace structure, test commands per package, root scripts, the release workflow (version sync chain), and the server daemon entry point.

nodes.name: constitution.md
nodes.summary: Plugin constitution injected at session start via bootstrap.sh defining the four Karpathy principles (Think Before Coding, Simplicity First, Surgical Changes, Goal-Driven Execution) and project-specific rules including mandatory TDD, verification before done, and escalation policies.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Design contract for the spec-viewer skill's DocsPage component, specifying design tokens (colors, typography, spacing), tab component patterns, markdown rendering CSS class conventions, right sidebar layout, and aesthetic principles.

nodes.name: extract-changelog.mjs
nodes.summary: Release utility script that parses CHANGELOG.md and extracts the release notes body for a given version tag, used by the GitHub Actions release workflow to populate GitHub Release descriptions.

nodes.name: extractChangelog
nodes.summary: Parses CHANGELOG.md content line by line to extract and return the body of the section matching a given version tag, stopping at the next H2 heading.

nodes.name: sync-version.mjs
nodes.summary: Release utility script that reads the authoritative version from root package.json and propagates it to all workspace package.json files, plugin/plugin.json, and the VERSION constant in packages/server/src/index.ts. Invoked by release-it after each version bump.

nodes.name: syncVersion
nodes.summary: Reads the version from the root package.json and writes it to all derived locations: server/web/shared package.json files, plugin/plugin.json, and the VERSION string literal in packages/server/src/index.ts.

nodes.name: bootstrap.sh
nodes.summary: Claude Code SessionStart hook that probes for a running daemon via runtime.json and healthz endpoint, starts it if absent, and emits the plugin constitution (merged with any project-specific constitution) as session context to Claude.

nodes.name: forward.sh
nodes.summary: Lightweight Claude Code hook that reads the daemon port and token from runtime.json and forwards incoming hook event payloads to the daemon via HTTP POST, enabling real-time session event streaming to the dashboard.

nodes.name: ensure-daemon.sh
nodes.summary: Shared library script for plugin commands that ensures the claude-control daemon is running by probing its healthz endpoint and starting it from the known_marketplaces.json install location if not responsive.

nodes.name: stories
nodes.summary: Migration adding a seq INTEGER column to the stories table and a partial unique index idx_stories_seq to support incremental US{n} story ID generation.

nodes.name: handoff.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code plugin command definition for the handoff workflow, instructing Claude to summarize work done, outstanding tasks, and context needed for the next session or developer.

nodes.name: backlog.md
nodes.summary: Sub-command handler for the start command when the active story is in Backlog status, guiding Claude to present the story and prompt the user to begin work.

nodes.name: done.md
nodes.summary: Sub-command handler for the start command when the active story is in Done status, providing a completion report by comparing git log against acceptance criteria.

nodes.name: in-progress.md
nodes.summary: Sub-command handler for the start command when the active story is In Progress, fetching the linked plan and generating a progress report from git log and acceptance criteria.

nodes.name: open.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code plugin command definition for the open command, instructing Claude to print the Claude Control dashboard URL for the browser.

nodes.name: plan.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code plugin command definition for the plan command, guiding Claude to generate a step-by-step implementation plan for the active story and save it to the docs directory.

nodes.name: spec.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code plugin command definition for the spec command, guiding Claude to write a technical design spec for the active story and save it to the docs directory.

nodes.name: standup.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code plugin command definition for the standup command, instructing Claude to generate a brief daily standup summary covering yesterday's progress, today's plan, and blockers.

nodes.name: start.md
nodes.summary: Primary entry-point command for the start workflow, dispatching to status-specific sub-handlers (backlog, in-progress, done) after loading and validating the active story.

nodes.name: status.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code plugin command definition for the status command, instructing Claude to report the daemon health, current active story, and inferred development phase.

nodes.name: story.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code plugin command definition for the story command, providing subcommands to create, list, and size user stories stored in the database.

nodes.name: 2026-05-25-realtime-story-sync.md
nodes.summary: Detailed implementation plan for real-time story synchronization, covering 5 tasks: title sync via upsertRow, watcher deletion branch, startup pruning in loadAll, a reconcile() periodic diff method, and interval lifecycle wiring.

nodes.name: 2026-05-29-stories-refresh-button.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for adding a manual refresh button and last-updated timestamp to the Stories board, covering CSS additions, FilterBar props, and React Query wiring in StoriesPage.

nodes.name: 2026-06-02-versioning-and-release.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive implementation plan for the versioning and release mechanism, spanning 9 tasks including baseline version, changelog scripts, sync-version script, release-it configuration, status API endpoint, dashboard UI, and GitHub Actions workflows.

nodes.name: 2026-06-04-spec-plan-viewer-docs-page.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for the Spec/Plan Viewer DocsPage, covering 8 tasks: extending HierarchyStrip and LinkedCard, building DocsPage with TDD, updating routing, Sidebar, StoryPage, CSS, deleting old pages, and creating a spec-viewer agent skill.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-start-command-status-branching.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for the start command status-branching refactor, detailing 4 tasks to create the three mode sub-files (backlog, in-progress, done) and update start.md to dispatch to them.

nodes.name: 2026-06-10-story-id-incremental.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for incremental US{n} story ID generation, covering a database migration to add a seq column, replacing STORY_ID_REGEX with isValidStoryId(), and rewriting create() to use COALESCE(MAX(seq), 0) + 1.

nodes.name: 2026-05-25-realtime-story-sync-design.md
nodes.summary: Technical design specification for real-time story synchronization, defining the watcher callback deletion branch, upsertRow title sync, loadAll startup pruning, reconcile() periodic diff, and interval lifecycle with data flow diagrams.

nodes.name: 2026-05-29-stories-refresh-button-design.md
nodes.summary: Design specification for the Stories board refresh button and last-updated timestamp, covering placement, UI behavior, CSS additions, and component-level changes to StoriesPage and FilterBar.

nodes.name: 2026-06-02-versioning-release-design.md
nodes.summary: Design specification for the versioning and release mechanism, defining the version source of truth, sync script, release-it workflow, GitHub Actions CI and release pipelines, plugin manifest, and CHANGELOG bootstrap.

nodes.name: 2026-06-04-spec-plan-viewer-design.md
nodes.summary: Design specification for the Spec/Plan Viewer DocsPage component, defining architecture, routing, component layout (spec/plan tabs, sidebar), the spec-viewer agent skill, error states, and testing approach.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-start-command-status-branching-design.md
nodes.summary: Design specification for status-based branching in the start command, defining the three mode files (backlog, in-progress, done) and the dispatch step added to start.md.

nodes.name: 2026-06-10-story-id-incremental-design.md
nodes.summary: Design specification for incremental US{n} story IDs, covering the problem with date-slug IDs, the seq column migration approach, changes to StoryService create() and isValidStoryId(), and boundary behavior.

nodes.name: US-2026-05-23-fix-auth-stop-exposing-token-via-url-in-.md
nodes.summary: User story for fixing a security issue where the auth token was exposed via URL query parameter, with acceptance criteria requiring token transmission via Authorization header instead.

nodes.name: US-2026-06-01-implement-versioning-and-release-mechani.md
nodes.summary: User story for implementing the versioning and release mechanism, with acceptance criteria covering version sync, CHANGELOG, release-it workflow, and GitHub Actions pipelines.

nodes.name: US-2026-06-02-plugin-parse-superpowers-spec-plan-files.md
nodes.summary: User story for making the plugin parse and display superpowers spec and plan files, with acceptance criteria for the DocsPage viewer and spec-viewer agent skill.

nodes.name: US-2026-06-08-optimize-story-id-generation-to-use-incr.md
nodes.summary: User story for replacing date-slug story IDs with short incremental US{n} IDs, with acceptance criteria for the seq column migration and updated ID validation logic.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Server entry point that exports `startDaemon`, which bootstraps the entire daemon: runs DB migrations, initialises the event bus, starts the story service and superpowers watcher, binds the HTTP server to a free port, manages idle shutdown, and writes the runtime.json lock file.

nodes.name: startDaemon
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the daemon: creates runtime directories, runs DB migrations, loads or generates an auth token, wires all subsystems (bus, stories, superpowers watcher, HTTP server, WebSocket server), manages idle shutdown timers, and writes the runtime.json file.

nodes.name: server.ts
nodes.summary: Provides the `createServer` factory that wraps `Bun.serve` and routes incoming HTTP requests — serving the static dashboard, forwarding hook events, delegating API calls, and managing WebSocket upgrades.

nodes.name: createServer
nodes.summary: Creates and returns a `Bun.serve` instance that handles static asset serving, WebSocket upgrade, hook event ingestion (with rate-limiting), and authenticated API routing.

nodes.name: bus.ts
nodes.summary: Implements a lightweight in-process publish/subscribe event bus used to propagate domain events (story updates, session changes, superpowers events) between subsystems without direct coupling.

nodes.name: createBus
nodes.summary: Returns a new bus object with `publish` and `subscribe` methods backed by a per-topic Set of handlers, allowing any subsystem to broadcast or listen for typed events.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: API router that dispatches incoming requests by URL path prefix to sub-route handlers for sessions, stories, superpowers, standup, and handoff endpoints.

nodes.name: mountApiRoutes
nodes.summary: Dispatches authenticated API requests to the appropriate sub-route handler based on URL prefix, returning 404 when no route matches.

nodes.name: sessions.ts
nodes.summary: Handles session-related HTTP routes: listing sessions, retrieving the active session, fetching session events with pagination, and posting session start events that update the DB and broadcast via the bus.

nodes.name: mountSessionRoutes
nodes.summary: Handles all `/api/sessions` routes: GET list, GET active, GET session by id, GET events with SSE-friendly pagination, and POST to register a new session start.

nodes.name: handoff.ts
nodes.summary: Mounts API routes for the handoff feature, allowing listing of handoff documents and on-demand generation of a handoff for a specific story ID.

nodes.name: mountHandoffRoutes
nodes.summary: Handles `/api/handoff` GET (list) and `/api/handoff/:id` GET (generate for story), validating the story ID before generation.

nodes.name: handlers.ts
nodes.summary: Processes Claude Code hook events by persisting them to the database and publishing them on the event bus so connected WebSocket clients receive real-time updates.

nodes.name: dispatchEvent
nodes.summary: Persists a hook event to the database then publishes it on the bus with both a generic `event` topic and a session-scoped topic for targeted WebSocket fan-out.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the `StoryService` class which manages the full lifecycle of stories: CRUD operations backed by SQLite, file-system watching for markdown story files, periodic reconciliation, and publishing events on the bus.

nodes.name: StoryService
nodes.summary: Core domain service managing story entities: persists to SQLite, watches the stories directory for file changes, performs periodic reconciliation to sync DB with disk, and publishes lifecycle events via the bus.

nodes.name: isValidStoryId
nodes.summary: Validates that a story ID matches either the legacy `US-YYYY-MM-DD-slug` or new incremental `USn` format.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Defines `SuperpowersWatcher` which monitors plan and spec directories for file changes, parses plan markdown to detect task checkbox diffs, auto-links files to active stories, and advances the inferred development phase on the session.

nodes.name: SuperpowersWatcher
nodes.summary: Watches plan and spec directories, parses markdown plan files for checkbox state, detects changes to derive progress, auto-links spec/plan files to the active story, and triggers phase advancement on the current session.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Implements `WsServer`, a WebSocket server that subscribes to the event bus and fans out messages to connected clients filtered by their subscribed topics, and handles authentication via a token-based protocol.

nodes.name: WsServer
nodes.summary: Manages WebSocket connections with per-socket topic subscriptions; subscribes to the event bus on construction and fans out typed events as JSON messages to clients subscribed to the relevant topic.

nodes.name: daemon.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for daemon startup and shutdown lifecycle, verifying that `startDaemon` writes the expected runtime.json and that the process cleans up on exit.

nodes.name: integration.test.ts
nodes.summary: End-to-end integration tests covering the full daemon stack — HTTP API, WebSocket events, story and session flows — running against a live `startDaemon` instance.

nodes.name: server.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the `createServer` HTTP layer, verifying routing, static file serving, auth enforcement, and hook event ingestion behaviour.

nodes.name: routes.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for all API route handlers, exercising session, story, superpowers, and handoff endpoints with a real SQLite in-memory database and a stub bus.

nodes.name: migrate.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests the SQLite migration runner, verifying idempotency, schema correctness, and forward migration ordering across all migration steps.

nodes.name: service.test.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive unit tests for `StoryService` covering create, update, archive, file watching reconciliation, and event bus publication behaviour.

nodes.name: watcher.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for `SuperpowersWatcher` covering plan parsing, checkbox diff detection, spec tracking, auto-linking, and phase advancement logic.

nodes.name: ws.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration-style tests for `WsServer` that connect a real WebSocket client, verify topic subscription, fan-out delivery, and auth token enforcement.

nodes.name: extract-changelog.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the `extract-changelog.mjs` release script, verifying that CHANGELOG entries are correctly parsed and extracted for a given version.

nodes.name: sync-version.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests the `sync-version.mjs` release script using a temporary repository fixture, asserting that version strings are propagated to all expected package.json and config files.

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript type definitions for all API response shapes and the `HookEvent` discriminated union used across client and server packages.

nodes.name: story.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the `Story` and `StoryFrontmatterSchema` Zod types and the `parseFrontmatter` utility used by both server and shared test code to decode YAML story file headers.

nodes.name: parseFrontmatter
nodes.summary: Parses the YAML frontmatter block from a markdown story file and validates it against `StoryFrontmatterSchema`, returning the typed result.

nodes.name: story.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for `parseFrontmatter` covering valid frontmatter, missing fields, extra fields, and edge-case YAML formatting.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: React application entry point that mounts the App component into the DOM root with React 18's createRoot API.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root application shell that initializes the WebSocket connection, sets up React Router with Sidebar and Topbar layout, and routes between StoriesPage, StoryPage, and DocsPage.

nodes.name: Shell
nodes.summary: Inner layout component that calls useWebSocket to initialize the WS connection and renders the Sidebar, Topbar, and routed page outlet.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Default export that wraps Shell in a BrowserRouter/QueryClientProvider tree.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global CSS stylesheet defining design tokens, layout primitives, and component styles for the dashboard including sidebar, topbar, kanban board, and story card theming.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines CSS custom properties (variables) as design tokens and uses utility classes alongside component-scoped styles.

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: Centralized API client that reads the server base URL from the WebSocket store and exposes typed fetch helpers for stories, specs, and plans.

nodes.name: apiFetch
nodes.summary: Authenticated fetch wrapper that prepends the server base URL and Bearer token, returning parsed JSON or throwing on HTTP errors.

nodes.name: useWebSocket.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook that manages a persistent WebSocket connection to the Claude Control daemon, handling reconnection with exponential backoff, real-time event dispatch via React Query invalidation, and session state synchronization.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses useRef to hold mutable WS and timeout references outside the React render cycle, avoiding stale closure issues during reconnect.

nodes.name: useWebSocket
nodes.summary: Exported hook that opens a WebSocket connection, subscribes to events (story-updated, phase-changed, session-changed), invalidates React Query caches on events, and reconnects automatically with exponential backoff.

nodes.name: StoriesPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Kanban board page that fetches all stories, groups them by status into droppable columns, and supports drag-and-drop reordering with optimistic status mutation.

nodes.name: StoriesPage
nodes.summary: Page component implementing the Kanban board: fetches stories, sets up DnD context, applies optimistic mutation for drag-end status changes, and renders FilterBar and KanbanColumn children.

nodes.name: StoryPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Detail page for a single user story that displays parsed acceptance criteria, status/size selectors with optimistic mutations, and linked spec/plan document cards.

nodes.name: parseUserStory
nodes.summary: Pure utility that parses a markdown story body into a structured object containing the user-story sentence and a list of acceptance criteria items with their checked state.

nodes.name: StoryPage
nodes.summary: Page component that loads a story by route param, renders its title, status/size controls with inline mutation, parsed acceptance criteria checklist, and LinkedCard widgets for spec and plan documents.

nodes.name: DocsPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Documentation viewer page that loads spec and plan files for the active story, renders a HierarchyStrip breadcrumb, plan task progress, and LinkedCard navigation between spec and plan views.

nodes.name: DocsPage
nodes.summary: Page component that uses the active story from WS store and search params to fetch and render spec/plan documents, showing task progress and navigation cards.

nodes.name: Sidebar.tsx
nodes.summary: Navigation sidebar component displaying the active story, connection status indicator, and facet navigation tabs (story/docs) that update the current route.

nodes.name: Sidebar
nodes.summary: Sidebar component that fetches the active story, renders WS connection status, story title, facet tabs navigating to story/docs routes, and a back-to-stories link.

nodes.name: Topbar.tsx
nodes.summary: Top navigation bar displaying the current session phase as a stepper, session ID, and connection status badge sourced from the WebSocket store.

nodes.name: Topbar
nodes.summary: Renders a phase stepper across all defined phases, highlights the active phase, and shows the truncated session ID and connection status.

nodes.name: HierarchyStrip.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable breadcrumb component that renders a horizontal strip of navigable nodes (label + path pairs) separated by chevrons.

nodes.name: HierarchyStrip
nodes.summary: Renders a breadcrumb strip from a list of label/path nodes, navigating programmatically on click.

nodes.name: LinkedCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable card component that renders a navigable document link with an icon, filename, subtitle, and optional progress bar.

nodes.name: LinkedCard
nodes.summary: Renders a clickable card with icon, filename, subtitle text, optional progress indicator, and active highlight state that navigates to the given route on click.

nodes.name: FilterBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Kanban filter bar component providing status filter pill buttons, a refresh action, fetching indicator, and last-updated timestamp.

nodes.name: FilterBar
nodes.summary: Renders a row of filter pills for story status, per-status count badges, a refresh button with loading state, and a formatted last-updated time.

nodes.name: KanbanColumn.tsx
nodes.summary: Droppable Kanban column component that renders a status group header and a list of StoryCard items, with a filter-by-status shortcut.

nodes.name: KanbanColumn
nodes.summary: Renders a droppable column for a given story status, showing a count badge and a list of draggable StoryCard children; clicking the header sets the active story filter.

nodes.name: StoryCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Draggable story card component rendering a story's ID, title, size badge, and active-session indicator, navigating to the StoryPage on click.

nodes.name: StoryCard
nodes.summary: Renders a draggable card for a story with ID slug, title, size chip, and active-story highlight based on WS store state; navigates to the story detail page on click.

nodes.name: test-setup.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest test setup file that configures the test environment globals (e.g. happy-dom or jsdom imports) for the web package.

nodes.name: DocsPage.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for DocsPage that mocks the API and WS store, verifying spec/plan document rendering, tab switching, and task progress display.

nodes.name: FilterBar.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for FilterBar verifying pill rendering, count badges, refresh callback invocation, and fetching indicator display.

nodes.name: Sidebar.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for Sidebar verifying active story display, connection status rendering, and facet navigation link behavior.

nodes.name: StoriesPage.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for StoriesPage verifying kanban column rendering, story card presence, and filter state integration.

nodes.name: Topbar.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for Topbar verifying phase stepper rendering and connection status badge display.

nodes.name: useWebSocket.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for the useWebSocket hook that uses a MockWebSocket class to simulate connection lifecycle, message dispatch, and reconnection backoff behavior.

nodes.name: MockWebSocket
nodes.summary: Test double for the browser WebSocket API that records sent messages and exposes helpers to simulate open, close, and receive events.

layers.name: Claude Code Plugin
layers.description: Observer-only Claude Code plugin layer including the plugin manifest, slash command definitions, lifecycle hooks (bootstrap and forward shell scripts), skill definitions, and daemon management utilities.

layers.name: Shared Types
layers.description: Cross-package TypeScript type definitions and utilities consumed by both the server and web packages, including the Zod-validated HookEvent schema, story frontmatter types, plan parser, and the typed API response shapes.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: SQLite migration files defining the core relational schema for sessions, events, stories, plan tasks, plan steps, handoffs, and incremental story ID generation.

layers.name: Server Daemon
layers.description: Bun HTTP server daemon containing the API route handlers, WebSocket server, hook event processing, story and superpowers file watchers, SQLite data store helpers, migration runner, security middleware, lifecycle management, and the startDaemon entry point.

layers.name: Client State & Services
layers.description: Zustand stores managing UI filter and WebSocket connection state, the useWebSocket reconnection hook, and the typed HTTP API client used by all React pages to communicate with the daemon.

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: React dashboard SPA including the application entry point, router shell, layout components (Sidebar, Topbar), all page views (Stories, Story, Docs, Standup), and shared UI primitives such as KanbanColumn, StoryCard, FilterBar, TaskCard, and pill badges.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: All test files across the server, shared, and web packages including unit tests for services and utilities, integration tests for the live daemon stack, and component tests for the React dashboard.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Monorepo build and tooling configuration including root workspace manifests, TypeScript base config, Biome linter settings, release-it configuration, version sync and changelog extraction scripts, web build tooling (Vite, Tailwind, PostCSS), Claude Code project settings, and CI/CD GitHub Actions pipelines.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project-level documentation including the PRD, design system reference, implementation plans, technical specs, user stories, handoff documents, CHANGELOG, CLAUDE.md project guide, and static HTML prototype assets.

tour.title: Project Overview & Plugin Model
tour.description: The PRD establishes what Claude Control is: an observer-only plugin that sits alongside Claude Code and provides an agile workflow layer — story tracking, real-time dashboards, and standup reports — without interfering with Claude's behavior. Reading it first explains every design decision you will encounter throughout the codebase: why the server is a daemon rather than a long-running process, why hooks are read-only, and how the three surfaces (daemon, dashboard, plugin) relate to each other.

tour.title: Plugin Manifest & Hook Registration
tour.description: The plugin manifest declares every entry point Claude Code loads: commands, hooks, and skills. The companion hooks.json maps all Claude Code lifecycle events — SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, and more — to lightweight shell scripts that forward the JSON payload to the daemon. This is the seam between Claude Code and the rest of the system: every piece of data the dashboard displays originates from these hook invocations.
tour.languageLesson: Claude Code hook scripts are bash shims that receive the event payload on stdin and must exit quickly. They delegate the real work to the daemon by POSTing to a localhost HTTP endpoint, keeping the observer-only contract intact.

tour.title: Server Daemon Entry Point
tour.description: The server's `startDaemon` export is the bootstrap sequence for the entire backend: it runs SQL migrations, initialises the event bus, starts the StoryService and SuperpowersWatcher, binds to the first available port in 47821–47830, registers SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers, and writes a runtime.json lock file so the plugin scripts know where to connect. Understanding this file gives you a mental map of every subsystem you will encounter in the following steps.
tour.languageLesson: Bun's single-binary runtime means `startDaemon` can import both the HTTP server and SQLite driver without a build step. The function returns a cleanup handle so tests can tear down the server deterministically.

tour.title: Database Schema
tour.description: The SQLite migrations define the core data model in four incremental files. The initial migration establishes `sessions` (one per Claude Code invocation) and `events` (every hook payload, stored as JSON). The second adds `stories`, `plan_tasks`, and `plan_steps` for the agile layer. The third adds `handoffs`, and the fourth adds a `seq` column to support incremental US{n} story ID generation. Every API response and WebSocket message ultimately draws from these tables.
tour.languageLesson: SQLite migrations should be idempotent and applied in sorted filename order. The `_migrations` tracking table records which files have already run, so the runner can safely re-execute on every daemon start without duplicating schema changes.

tour.title: Migration Runner & Store Helpers
tour.description: The migration runner reads SQL files from disk in sorted order and applies any not yet recorded in the `_migrations` table — it is called on every daemon startup so the schema is always current. The store module then provides the write helpers that all subsystems use: `upsertSession`, `endSession`, and `persistEvent`. Together these two files form the entire data layer: one sets up the schema, the other writes to it.

tour.title: Shared Types Package
tour.description: With 25 fan-in edges, `packages/shared/src/index.ts` is the most-depended-upon file in the entire codebase — it is the contract between the server and the web frontend. The `events.ts` module defines the Zod-validated discriminated union for every Claude Code hook event type, while `plan.ts` defines `ParsedPlan`, `PlanTask`, and `PlanStep` along with the `parsePlan` markdown parser. Any TypeScript file that touches events or plan documents imports from here.
tour.languageLesson: Zod discriminated unions validate external JSON at runtime while simultaneously providing TypeScript types at compile time. The `z.discriminatedUnion('hookEventName', [...])` pattern means each hook type has its own narrow payload shape, preventing accidental access to fields that don't exist on a given event.

tour.title: In-Process Event Bus
tour.description: The event bus is the internal nervous system of the server: every subsystem — hook handler, story service, superpowers watcher — publishes domain events here, and the WebSocket server subscribes to fan them out to connected dashboard clients. With 13 fan-in edges it is second only to the shared package in importance, yet its implementation is intentionally minimal — a lightweight pub/sub with no external dependencies — because decoupling is the goal, not feature richness.
tour.languageLesson: An in-process event bus trades the reliability guarantees of a message broker for zero latency and zero infrastructure. This trade-off is appropriate here because all consumers live in the same Bun process; if the daemon restarts, WebSocket clients reconnect and re-sync.

tour.title: HTTP Server & Request Routing
tour.description: The `createServer` factory wraps `Bun.serve` and acts as the single ingress point for the daemon: static dashboard assets are served from `dist/`, hook event POSTs are forwarded to the hook handler, WebSocket upgrade requests are handed off to WsServer, and all other `/api/*` routes pass through the security middleware before reaching the API router. The security module enforces bearer-token authentication on every non-public route and applies per-route rate limiting.

tour.title: Hook Event Handler
tour.description: When a Claude Code hook fires, the plugin's shell script POSTs to `/hooks` and this module handles the rest: it parses and Zod-validates the payload against the shared `HookEventSchema`, triggers SuperpowersWatcher's file-change detection for Write events, and dispatches the validated event to both the SQLite store and the event bus. This is the critical path for all real-time data — every session, event, and story update begins here.

tour.title: API Router & Session Endpoints
tour.description: The API router dispatches incoming requests by URL prefix to sub-routers for sessions, stories, superpowers, standup, and handoff. The sessions sub-router handles the most fundamental operations: listing sessions, retrieving the active session, paginating session events, and accepting the session-start POST that the plugin fires on `SessionStart`. Together, these two files expose the primary read surface that the web dashboard queries on load.

tour.title: Story Service & Superpowers Watcher
tour.description: The `StoryService` is the heart of the agile layer: it manages story CRUD backed by SQLite, watches the filesystem for markdown story files, runs periodic reconciliation to keep the DB in sync with disk, and publishes events on the bus when stories change. The `SuperpowersWatcher` complements it by monitoring plan and spec directories — it parses plan markdown to detect checkbox state diffs using the `diff.ts` utility, auto-links spec and plan files to active stories, and advances the inferred development phase.

tour.title: WebSocket Server & Daemon Lifecycle
tour.description: The `WsServer` subscribes to the event bus and fans out messages to connected dashboard clients, filtering by each client's subscribed topics and requiring token-based authentication in the WebSocket handshake protocol. The lifecycle module handles everything else that keeps the daemon running cleanly: writing the runtime.json PID file, managing the idle-shutdown timer that stops the server after inactivity, and registering graceful SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers.

tour.title: Dashboard Entry Point & Client State
tour.description: The React application mounts from `main.tsx` into the DOM root, then `App.tsx` takes over: it initializes the WebSocket connection, sets up React Router with the persistent Sidebar and Topbar layout, and routes between the four page views. Client state lives in two Zustand stores — `ws.ts` holds WebSocket connection status and server base URL (read by the API client), while `ui.ts` holds the active story filter for the Kanban board. The `useWebSocket` hook manages reconnection with exponential backoff and dispatches real-time events as React Query cache invalidations.
tour.languageLesson: Zustand stores are plain functions with `set`/`get` closures — no reducers, no actions, no boilerplate. The WebSocket store exposes the server base URL so the API client can be constructed lazily at call time rather than at module load, allowing the URL to be resolved after the daemon announces its port.

tour.title: API Client & Dashboard Pages
tour.description: The `api.ts` module is a typed fetch wrapper that reads the base URL from the WebSocket Zustand store and provides helpers for every server endpoint. The four page components built on top of it cover the full product surface: `StoriesPage` renders a Kanban board with drag-and-drop status mutation, `StoryPage` shows parsed acceptance criteria and linked spec/plan cards, `DocsPage` renders spec/plan documents with task progress, and `StandupPage` generates a daily digest with a clipboard export action.

tour.title: CI Pipeline & Design System
tour.description: The GitHub Actions CI workflow runs on every push and pull request: it installs dependencies with Bun and executes both the server test suite (bun:test) and the web test suite (vitest) in sequence. The DESIGN.md design system document provides the visual vocabulary used throughout the dashboard — a Supabase-inspired palette, typography scale, component patterns, and an agent prompt guide for generating new UI consistently. Together, these two files represent the quality gates: automated correctness via CI, and visual consistency via the design system.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows run `on: [push, pull_request]` triggers. The monorepo uses a single workflow file that runs both `bun test` (server, bun:test runner) and `bun run test` (web, vitest runner) as separate steps — these must not be swapped since each package uses a different test runner.
